classification–best.R

diyashaweersuriya

2025-03-21

library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(ggplot2)
library(class)
library(caret)
## Warning: package 'caret' was built under R version 4.3.3
## Loading required package: lattice
library(e1071)
## Warning: package 'e1071' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
## Warning: package 'rpart.plot' was built under R version 4.3.3
# Load Data
data <- read.csv("online_shoppers_intention.csv", header = TRUE)

# Check column names
print(colnames(data))
##  [1] "Administrative"          "Administrative_Duration"
##  [3] "Informational"           "Informational_Duration" 
##  [5] "ProductRelated"          "ProductRelated_Duration"
##  [7] "BounceRates"             "ExitRates"              
##  [9] "PageValues"              "SpecialDay"             
## [11] "Month"                   "OperatingSystems"       
## [13] "Browser"                 "Region"                 
## [15] "TrafficType"             "VisitorType"            
## [17] "Weekend"                 "Revenue"
# Selecting Relevant Columns for Classification
Class_data <- data[, c("Administrative", "Administrative_Duration", "Informational", 
                       "Informational_Duration", "ProductRelated", "ProductRelated_Duration", 
                       "BounceRates", "ExitRates", "PageValues", "SpecialDay")]

# Add categorical target variable (Revenue) for classification
Class_data$Revenue <- as.factor(data$Revenue)

# Check for and handle missing values
Class_data <- na.omit(Class_data)

# Normalization Function
normalize <- function(x) {
  (x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x))
}

# Normalizing Numeric Features
Class_data_n <- as.data.frame(lapply(Class_data[, 1:10], normalize))
Class_data_n$Revenue <- Class_data$Revenue

# Splitting into Train and Test Sets
set.seed(123)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(Class_data_n$Revenue, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train <- Class_data_n[trainIndex, ]
test <- Class_data_n[-trainIndex, ]

# K-Value Optimization
set.seed(123)
k_values <- seq(1, 20, by = 2)
accuracy <- sapply(k_values, function(k) {
  pred <- knn(train[, 1:10], test[, 1:10], train$Revenue, k)
  mean(pred == test$Revenue)
})

# Plotting K-Value vs Accuracy
ggplot(data.frame(k = k_values, accuracy = accuracy), aes(x = k, y = accuracy)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("K-Value vs Accuracy") +
  xlab("K") +
  ylab("Accuracy")

# Optimal K for KNN Model
k_optimal <- k_values[which.max(accuracy)]
knn_model <- knn(train[, 1:10], test[, 1:10], train$Revenue, k = k_optimal)
knn_accuracy <- mean(knn_model == test$Revenue)
print(paste("KNN Accuracy:", knn_accuracy))
## [1] "KNN Accuracy: 0.888858842617631"
# Confusion Matrix for KNN
knn_cm <- confusionMatrix(knn_model, test$Revenue)
print(knn_cm)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction FALSE TRUE
##      FALSE  3055  340
##      TRUE     71  232
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.8889          
##                  95% CI : (0.8783, 0.8988)
##     No Information Rate : 0.8453          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1.325e-14       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.4739          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : < 2.2e-16       
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.9773          
##             Specificity : 0.4056          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.8999          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.7657          
##              Prevalence : 0.8453          
##          Detection Rate : 0.8261          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.9181          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.6914          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : FALSE           
## 
ggplot(as.data.frame(knn_cm$table), aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  ggtitle("KNN Confusion Matrix")

# Naive Bayes Model
nb_model <- naiveBayes(Revenue ~ ., data = train)
nb_pred <- predict(nb_model, test)
nb_accuracy <- mean(nb_pred == test$Revenue)
print(paste("Naive Bayes Accuracy:", nb_accuracy))
## [1] "Naive Bayes Accuracy: 0.809356408869659"
# Confusion Matrix for Naive Bayes
nb_cm <- confusionMatrix(nb_pred, test$Revenue)
print(nb_cm)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction FALSE TRUE
##      FALSE  2626  205
##      TRUE    500  367
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.8094          
##                  95% CI : (0.7963, 0.8219)
##     No Information Rate : 0.8453          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 1               
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.3978          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : <2e-16          
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.8401          
##             Specificity : 0.6416          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.9276          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.4233          
##              Prevalence : 0.8453          
##          Detection Rate : 0.7101          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.7655          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7408          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : FALSE           
## 
ggplot(as.data.frame(nb_cm$table), aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  ggtitle("Naive Bayes Confusion Matrix")

# Decision Tree Model
dt_model <- rpart(Revenue ~ ., data = train, method = "class", control = rpart.control(cp = 0.005))
rpart.plot(dt_model)

dt_pred <- predict(dt_model, test, type = "class")
dt_accuracy <- mean(dt_pred == test$Revenue)
print(paste("Decision Tree Accuracy:", dt_accuracy))
## [1] "Decision Tree Accuracy: 0.898323418063818"
# Confusion Matrix for Decision Tree
dt_cm <- confusionMatrix(dt_pred, test$Revenue)
print(dt_cm)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction FALSE TRUE
##      FALSE  2992  242
##      TRUE    134  330
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.8983          
##                  95% CI : (0.8881, 0.9079)
##     No Information Rate : 0.8453          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : < 2.2e-16       
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.5787          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 3.427e-08       
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.9571          
##             Specificity : 0.5769          
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.9252          
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.7112          
##              Prevalence : 0.8453          
##          Detection Rate : 0.8091          
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.8745          
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.7670          
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : FALSE           
## 
ggplot(as.data.frame(dt_cm$table), aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  ggtitle("Decision Tree Confusion Matrix")

# Model Comparison
model_results <- data.frame(Model = c("KNN", "Naive Bayes", "Decision Tree"),
                            Accuracy = c(knn_accuracy, nb_accuracy, dt_accuracy))
print(model_results)
##           Model  Accuracy
## 1           KNN 0.8888588
## 2   Naive Bayes 0.8093564
## 3 Decision Tree 0.8983234
